notes.note: Pro quaestore: an officer holding a quaestor's financial authority in a province, either as an outgoing quaestor whose term ran on past the year, or as a deputy administering finances under the governor's imperium. Appuleius held this post in Asia.

Anchialus is the slave (servus) of the knight L. Egnatius, sent to Asia on his master's business; Cicero commends both the slave and the affairs as if they were his own --- a measure of how far a recommendation could be stretched on a friend's behalf.
